author: jamescliffordspratt
description: Nutrition and food tracker for your notes. Log meals, calories, macros and micronutrients. Search foods by name or barcode via FatSecret, USDA & Open Food Facts. Chart your diet & weight loss.

manifest.author: James Spratt
manifest.description: Nutrition and food tracker for your notes. Log meals, calories and macros (protein, fat, carbs) plus micronutrients. Search foods by name or barcode via FatSecret, USDA & Open Food Facts, set targets, and visualize with tables and charts.
manifest.name: Macros

name: Macros

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Macros 2.6.2

A polish-and-hardening release: a notable cold-start bug fix, food-table refinements, and a broad pass to meet Obsidian's community plugin scorecard (security, dependencies, CSS, and deprecated APIs).

## Fixes

- **Charts now render on startup.** When Obsidian reopened on a note containing a `macrospc` or `macroscalc` chart, the chart could appear blank until you switched notes and back. Charts now draw reliably on a cold start (they render as soon as the view becomes visible).
- **Centered the "Food" column header** so it lines up with the other column headers again.

## Food table

- **Removed the inline "−" remove-item button** from food cells. Remove a food using the right-click context menu on desktop, or press-and-hold on mobile. Dropping the button frees up horizontal space so food names display with less truncation.
- Improved food-name display and tightened the left spacing in the name column.

## Interface text

- Standardized English UI text to **sentence case** for consistency with Obsidian's interface conventions.

## Security & reliability

- **Removed the `crypto-js` dependency.** FatSecret request signing (HMAC-SHA1) now uses the browser's built-in Web Crypto API, clearing the associated security advisories.
- **Removed dynamic remote script loading** from the barcode scanner; the scanning library is bundled with the plugin instead.

## Under the hood

- Raised `minAppVersion` to **1.5.7** and removed the `builtin-modules` build dependency.
- CSS cleanup: eliminated `!important` overrides, switched scrollbar styling to fully-supported equivalents, replaced `:has()` selectors with class toggles, and removed duplicate declarations.
- Replaced the deprecated `setWarning()` API with the equivalent styling, preserving the red destructive-button look.
- Release artifacts (`main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`) are now published with build provenance attestations.

## Notes

Aside from the chart fix and the food-table layout changes, your existing macro tables, templates, charts, and food searches continue to work exactly as before. The only behavior change is how you remove a food from a table (right-click / press-and-hold instead of the inline button).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Macros 2.6.2

A maintenance release focused on the food table layout, security, and dependencies.

## Food table
- **Removed the inline "−" remove-item icon from food cells.** Removing a food now uses the right-click context menu on desktop, and press-and-hold on mobile. Dropping the icon frees up horizontal space so food names display with less truncation.
- Improved food-name display: names now use the reclaimed space and wrap/fit more cleanly, with reduced left padding.

## Security & dependencies
- **Dropped `crypto-js`.** FatSecret OAuth request signing (HMAC-SHA1) now uses the browser's built-in Web Crypto API, removing the `crypto-js` dependency and its associated security advisories.
- **Removed the `builtin-modules` dev dependency** in favor of Node's native `module.builtinModules`.

## Maintenance
- Raised `minAppVersion` to **1.5.7** to match the APIs the plugin uses.
- Build artifacts (`main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`) are now published with signed provenance attestations.
- Internal cleanup: documented lint directives and removed redundant duplicate CSS declarations.

## Notes
Existing macro tables, templates, charts, and food searches continue to work exactly as before. The only visible change is how you remove a food from a table (right-click / press-and-hold instead of the inline icon).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Macros v2.6.1

A maintenance and reliability release: faster food search, two notable bug fixes, and a full code-quality pass to meet Obsidian's new plugin review standards.

## 🐛 Bug fixes

- **Manual food entry — micronutrient section:** Fixed the collapsible micronutrient dropdown not opening when clicked. It now expands and collapses correctly.
- **Live search — "All" tab:** Results no longer vanish when one food database is slow or unavailable. Previously, if Open Food Facts errored, the entire "All" tab returned nothing even though FatSecret and USDA had results; a failing source is now skipped instead of blanking the whole tab.
- Fixed an edge-case error in search-result highlighting for queries containing special characters.

## ⚡ Performance

- **Much faster Open Food Facts search.** Searches now query the main (world) database directly instead of fanning out to several slow regional mirror endpoints — cutting each search from up to ~6 network requests down to 2. This speeds up both the Open Food Facts tab and the combined "All" tab.
- The "All" tab now gives each food source a time budget, so a slow or unresponsive API can no longer stall the whole search.

## 🔧 Under the hood

- **Full type-safety pass:** added proper TypeScript types for every food-database API response (FatSecret, USDA, Open Food Facts) and the internal data flow, removing unsafe `any` usage throughout.
- Brought the codebase fully in line with Obsidian's official plugin guidelines (now passes `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` with zero issues) and updated the build toolchain (TypeScript 5, latest typescript-eslint).
- Improved popout-window and cross-platform compatibility (`activeDocument` and the Obsidian `Platform` API).
- Moved inline styles into CSS classes for better theme and snippet compatibility.
- Set the minimum Obsidian version to **1.4.10** to match the APIs the plugin actually uses.

> No changes to your data or note format — this release is fully backward compatible.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 🥗 Macros v2.6.0 — Micronutrients everywhere & a faster, smarter search

This release brings full **micronutrient support** to your food searches and notes, and gives the search experience a serious speed and reliability boost.

### ✨ What's new

**Micronutrients from every database**
Vitamins and minerals are now pulled automatically from all integrated sources — **USDA FoodData Central**, **Open Food Facts**, and **FatSecret** — wherever the data exists. No more manual entry for foods that already have the numbers.

**See micronutrients right in your search results**
Foods that have micronutrient data now show a clean summary of their vitamins and minerals (per 100 g) directly in the results list, so you can compare options at a glance. Foods without recorded data stay tidy and clutter-free.

**Saved straight into your notes**
When you add a food, its micronutrients are written into the note's frontmatter *and* a readable breakdown section — correctly scaled to your serving size and ready for tracking and aggregation.

### ⚡ Search improvements

- **Faster results** — USDA lookups now run in parallel, and Open Food Facts only reaches out to extra regional servers when it actually needs more, cutting load times noticeably.
- **The "All" tab is fuller** — it now returns a complete page of results instead of a sparse handful.
- **Infinite scroll that actually works** — just keep scrolling to load more results on any tab; it stops cleanly when there's nothing left.
- **Instant tab switching** — results are cached per tab, so flipping between sources no longer triggers a slow reload.

### 🌍 Localization
Added micronutrient labels in **English, Spanish, and Simplified Chinese**.

---

**Note:** FatSecret micronutrient coverage depends on your account tier, and many community-contributed Open Food Facts entries simply don't have vitamin/mineral data recorded — in those cases nothing is shown or saved, which is expected.

💛 As always, thanks for using Macros! Found a bug or have an idea? [Open an issue](https://github.com/JamesCliffordSpratt/macros/issues).

releases.author: JamesCliffordSpratt
releases.description:
# 🥗 Macros v2.5.0 — Micronutrient Tracking
This release introduces a complete micronutrient tracking foundation — track vitamins, minerals, and other nutrients with personalized targets based on your age, sex, and life stage.
## ✨ What's New
## 💊 Micronutrient tracking
Track 32 nutrients across vitamins, minerals, and other commonly tracked values. Targets are derived from official RDA/AI Dietary Reference Intakes, automatically matched to your profile — including pregnancy and lactation life stages.
## ⚙️ New "Micronutrients" settings tab

A master enable toggle so tracking is fully opt-in
A personal profile (sex, age, life stage) that drives your recommended values
Per-nutrient editable targets — defaults to the recommendation, override any value, or reset individually or all at once

## 🍎 Optional micronutrient entry for foods
The manual food entry modal now includes a collapsible, optional micronutrient section. Pick nutrients from a category-grouped dropdown and they're saved into the food's frontmatter — no clutter unless you want it.
## 📊 Micronutrient summary in your macros tables
A micronutrient summary table now appears beneath your macros output, showing consumed vs. target with progress bars. It only shows up when tracking is enabled and your foods carry micronutrient data, so existing tables stay clean.
## 🌍 Localization
Full Spanish and Chinese translations for all new strings.
## 🛠️ Improvements & Fixes

Fixed an obscured dropdown in the manual entry modal by restoring native select rendering
New settings (micronutrientTrackingEnabled, micronutrientProfile, micronutrientTargets) ship with migration guards for a seamless upgrade


🔮 Coming next: Automatic micronutrient data from the food database APIs. The frontmatter scheme in this release is the foundation those fetchers will build on.

📦 Dependencies

Bumped lodash 4.17.21 → 4.18.1 (#30)
Bumped handlebars 4.7.8 → 4.7.9 (#31)
Bumped flatted 3.3.3 → 3.4.2 (#32)
Bumped minimatch (#33)
Bumped picomatch 2.3.1 → 2.3.2 (#34)

🔧 Maintenance

Added a GitHub Actions release workflow

Full Changelog: https://github.com/JamesCliffordSpratt/macros/compare/v2.4.0...v2.5.0

releases.author: JamesCliffordSpratt
releases.description:
🚀 Macros v2.4.0 – Smarter Tracking, Richer Data, and Barcode Scanning!

This update brings powerful new integrations and smarter tracking tools:
• USDA + Open Food Facts APIs for more accurate nutrition data
• Food tolerance tracking and timestamps for detailed logging
• Experimental barcode scanning using ZXing
• MacrosCalc metrics and streaks to visualize progress
• Fuzzy search for faster, smarter food lookups
Plus improved performance, smoother UI, and many quality-of-life tweaks.

releases.author: JamesCliffordSpratt
releases.description:
feat: enhance search UX and resolve CSS conflicts

### Search Improvements - (Suggested by @mathfen)
- Add clear button to AddToMacrosModal search input with mobile-optimized sizing
- Support both click and Escape key to clear search
- Dynamic visibility (only shows when text is present)

### CSS Conflict Resolution #22 
- Scope .menu styles to plugin context menus using data-macros-plugin attribute
- Fix mobile hamburger menu size regression introduced in v2.3.0
- Prevent plugin styles from affecting Obsidian's native UI elements

### Code Quality
- Resolve all ESLint errors and warnings
- Complete missing translation strings for better i18n support

releases.author: JamesCliffordSpratt
releases.description:
✨ New Features:
- 🍽️ **Meal Groups**: Create organized collections of foods with the new `group:` syntax — perfect for organizing ingredients by category (proteins, vegetables, snacks, etc.) or custom meal arrangements
- 🗨️ **Comment System**: Add personal notes or remarks to meals and food items using the intuitive right-click context menu and comment modal

  ![Comments Demo](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JamesCliffordSpratt/macros/main/images/V2/Comments-Demo.gif)

- 💡 **Smart ID Suggestion**: Auto-suggests available food or meal IDs when editing macros blocks for faster input and reduced typos

🧰 **UI/UX Improvements**
- 🔍 **Enhanced Add to Macros Modal**:
  - Meal group selection – easily add entire groups of foods at once
  - Auto-focus on input field for quicker entry
  - Tab sorting toggle in plugin settings for customized workflow
- 📱 **Mobile Experience**: Improved long-press interactions and context menu positioning on touch devices
- 🎨 **Scoped CSS Styles**: [#20](https://github.com/JamesCliffordSpratt/macros/issues/20) – Prevented global style conflicts by namespacing all plugin UI classes

### 🌍 Localization
- Minor updates to translations (`en`, `es`, `zh-CN`)

---

Thanks to everyone testing and suggesting improvements!

releases.author: JamesCliffordSpratt
releases.description:
📦 Release: Localization Update & zh-CN Translation Support
🌐 New Features:
- Added full Chinese (zh-CN) translation to the plugin, thanks to the incredible contribution by [@Moyf](https://github.com/Moyf)!
- Chinese UI now uses culturally natural phrasing, accurate nutritional terminology, and appropriate formatting for numbers and units.
- Added "zh-CN" to supported locales and updated localization manager and translation index accordingly.

🧠 Improvements
- Refined label formatting: added space between values and units (e.g., 100g → 100 g) for clarity and SI compliance.
- Reordered word structure in tooltips for natural Chinese reading (e.g., 占 7% · 剩余 95.2 g).
- UI alignment tweaks for energy input rows (using justify-content: flex-end) and bold styling for Calories/Energy labels for better visibility.

🛠 Fixes
- Fixed pathing issue in ChartManager import (was ./../../main, now corrected to ../main).

Minor style adjustments based on feedback for improved cross-locale consistency.

🙏 Special Thanks
Huge thanks to [@Moyf](https://github.com/Moyf) for the thoughtful and high-quality Chinese translation!
Your attention to linguistic nuance, cultural formatting, and UI consistency has made this release significantly better. Your notes, CSS suggestions, and terminology insights were invaluable!

releases.author: JamesCliffordSpratt
releases.description:
📦 v2.2.0 – Localization, kJ Support, and Bug Fixes
This release introduces localization (i18n) support, a new energy unit (kJ), and resolves a bug related to modified meal templates in macros calculations.

✨ New Features
🌐 Localization Support [#11](https://github.com/JamesCliffordSpratt/macros/issues/11)
Full internationalization (i18n) support has been added.

All modals, settings, and UI text are now translatable via the /lang folder.

Contributions welcome!
Thanks to @Moyf and @mathfen for offering to help translate into Chinese and French.

⚡ Energy Unit: kJ [#13](https://github.com/JamesCliffordSpratt/macros/issues/13)
Manual food entries now support kilojoules (kJ) as an alternative energy unit.

kJ input is automatically converted and stored as calories internally.

🐛 Bug Fixes
🧮 Meal Template Calculation Fix [#12](https://github.com/JamesCliffordSpratt/macros/issues/12)
Resolved an issue where edited meal templates in macroscalc blocks used the original template values instead of user-modified values.

Now, the calculations correctly reflect any edits made to the code block, such as changes to quantities or foods.

Macros Plugin v2.0.0

Major overhaul of the plugin with a cleaner codebase, better performance, and new features:

- Style overhaul to better integrate with Obsidian theming
- Pie chart visualizations and trend line graphs now rendered using Chart.js
- Toggle on/off additional information within settings
- Smarter food search (highlighting + keyboard nav)

- Modular codebase
